title: papaver nudicaule subsp. rubroaurantiacum
intro: Papaver nudicaule subsp. rubroaurantiacum, a lesser-known cultivar of the poppy family, is a type of perennial flowering plant known for its vibrant, orange-red blooms. This plant contains alkaloids, including protopine and sanguinarine, which possess medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and inflammation. The health benefits of Papaver nudicaule subsp. rubroaurantiacum include its ability to alleviate coughs, reduce fever, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used for medicinal purposes, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
